summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy ’92 Oakland show highlighted by a scorching Shakedown Street and standout performances of Casey Jones, Stuck Inside of Mobile, Row Jimmy, Let It Grow and Dark Star.
review: The recording is a clean two‑track SBD from Stonebear that delivers crystal‑clear instrument separation and no noticeable dropouts. Musically the show is charged with the band “on” – Jerry’s guitar tone and vocals sparkle, Phil opens Shakedown Street with authority, Brent (Bobby) adds fiery keyboard riffs, and the setlist flows from a tight first set into a second half that peaks on Casey Jones and an incandescent Dark Star. While a few reviewers note a lukewarm Samson, the consensus is that this is one of the most memorable 1992 Bay Area dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating ~4.1 combined with an excellent soundboard recording and predominantly positive comments about the performance yields a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stonebear two‑track SBD is crisp, well‑balanced and free of major glitches; reviewers cite good matrix sources and no noticeable dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight, clean first set builds momentum that erupts in a second‑set high point with Casey Jones and an inspired Dark Star; the band feeds off each other and the audience throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeated praise for his guitar tone, solos (especially on Row Jimmy) and vocal performance; noted as ‘on’ all night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Opening notes on Shakedown Street highlighted as a strong start.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brent "Bobby" Mydland’s keyboard work described as fiery, with sick riffs and creative synth textures.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards praised for their energy and experimentation (MIDI smears into Dark Star).
